step_rationale: Leukocyte–platelet tethering receptor; mediates P-selectin-driven leukocyte rolling and platelet-leukocyte aggregation.
mechanism: PSGL-1 engages P-selectin on activated platelets, driving platelet-leukocyte crosstalk and prothrombotic amplification at the site of plaque rupture.

references.title: Abacavir causes leukocyte/platelet crosstalk by activating neutrophil P2X7 receptors thus releasing soluble lectin-like oxidized low-density lipoprotein receptor-1.
The effects of aspirin and ticagrelor on Toll-like receptor (TLR)-mediated platelet activation: results of a randomized, cross-over trial.
Platelet activation in patients with the Raynaud phenomenon.
Oligonucleotide microarray and QRT-PCR study of adhesion protein gene expression in acute coronary syndrome patients.
[P-selectin and its role in some diseases].
